evals.name: mixed-batch
evals.prompt: Just pushed: `feat(auth): add OAuth2 login with Google`. Also updated the existing UserService to support OAuth user creation alongside password users. Copy skills/triage-knowledge/evals/fixtures/user-service.md to docs/user-service.md before starting.
evals.expected_output: Triage searches docs/ and finds user-service.md as existing (→ sync). OAuth2 login is new (→ capture). Both classified with topology hints: user-service.md gets Connected/Tags output; OAuth item gets Suggested relations pointing at user-service.md.

assertions.description: Response contains '### Existing (→ sync)' section and user-service.md appears under it
Response contains '### New (→ capture)' section and OAuth2 login appears under it
Existing node entry includes 'Connected:' and 'Tags:' labels in the topology line
New item entry includes a 'Suggested relations:' line (value may be 'none')
Output contains the '**Autology** — Triage Results' header

evals.name: all-new-items
evals.prompt: Just pushed first commit for a new service: `feat(queue): add BullMQ job queue for background email sending`. No existing docs/ nodes relate to queues or email.
evals.expected_output: Triage searches docs/, finds no matches, uses the empty-state format. All items classified as New (→ capture). Suggested relations section present even if empty.

assertions.description: Response contains the exact phrase 'No existing matches found. All items classified as new.'
Each new item line includes the '— no matching node' suffix
BullMQ queue and/or email sending appears under '### New (→ capture)'
Each new item entry includes a 'Suggested relations:' line (value may be 'none')
Output contains the '**Autology** — Triage Results' header

evals.name: topology-hints-depth
evals.prompt: We just changed how the session-start hook works: it now reads the skill file path from an environment variable instead of computing it from BASH_SOURCE. docs/ already contains shell-hook-scripts.md — use it as-is.
evals.expected_output: Triage finds shell-hook-scripts.md as Existing. Topology hints include its wikilink connections (autology-internals, codeless-architecture-decision) and any shared-tag nodes. Connected nodes listed so sync knows what to check.

assertions.description: Topology line for shell-hook-scripts.md includes 'Connected:' label with autology-internals and codeless-architecture-decision as wikilinks
Topology line includes 'Hub', 'Orphan', or a link count (e.g. '2 links') for shell-hook-scripts.md
Output contains the '**Autology** — Triage Results' header
